summary: FreshGuard Smart Fridge System — a Shark Tank business pitch web app for FreshGuard Inc. Features a full pitch landing page with hero, company info, how-it-works, product features, target market, and financials ($500K for 15% equity, $3.3M valuation). Includes an interactive app demo mockup showing food tracking, expiration alerts, and recipe suggestions. Green color scheme (#2ECC71, #27AE60), modern responsive design with smooth animations.
architectural_notes: Single-page web app combining pitch presentation and interactive app demo mockup
Color scheme: greens (#2ECC71, #27AE60), clean whites, dark accents; responsive with animations
Financials: $500K ask for 15% equity, $3.3M valuation, COGS $18/unit, retail $79.99, $4.99/mo premium app
imageRequirements:
## Required Images
- FreshGuard logo combining a green leaf and a minimalist fridge silhouette on a white background, modern flat design style (freshguard-logo.dim_400x200.png)
- Hero background with soft green gradient and subtle geometric pattern of leaves and droplets, fresh and clean aesthetic (hero-bg.dim_1920x1080.png)
- FreshGuard smart fridge filter device product illustration, sleek white cylindrical device with green LED accents and sensor ports, on a white background (product-device.dim_600x600.png)

existing_features.title: Create a hero section at the top of the landing page featuring the FreshGuard logo (leaf + fridge icon design), the slogan 'Stay Fresh. Stay Smart. Waste Nothing.', and a call-to-action button. Use the green color scheme (#2ECC71, #27AE60) with clean whites and dark accents.

Add a company info and mission section displaying: Company name 'FreshGuard Inc.', mission statement 'To eliminate food waste and promote healthier, smarter kitchens through innovative technology that keeps food fresh longer and keeps families informed.', and team members: CEO Jordan Ellis (Product Vision & Strategy), CTO Maya Chen (Hardware & Software Engineering), COO Marcus Webb (Operations & Manufacturing).

Add a 'How It Works' section with 4 steps illustrated by emoji icons, covering: (1) Active carbon filter absorbs food odors, (2) Ethylene gas sensor detects spoiling food, (3) Barcode scanner tracks expiration dates, (4) Bluetooth chip sends alerts to phone and auto-releases mist cleanser when bad smells are detected.

Add a product features showcase section highlighting all 5 device features: Active carbon filter, Ethylene gas sensor, Barcode scanner for expiration dates, Bluetooth notifications, and Auto-mist cleanser. Use emoji icons and card-style layout.

Add a target market section describing: health-conscious families and busy professionals, ages 25–50, households spending $150+/month on groceries. Include the unique value proposition: 'Only device that COMBINES odor absorption + gas detection + expiration tracking + auto-mist cleanse in one unit.'

Add a Shark Tank-themed financials section with dramatic, investor-card styling. Display: Asking $500,000 for 15% equity, Company Valuation $3.3 Million, COGS $18/unit, Retail Price $79.99/unit, Markup 344%, App free with Premium subscription $4.99/month. Monthly expenses breakdown: Factory/Warehouse $8,000, Utilities $1,200, Advertising/Marketing $5,000, Employee Salaries (5 employees) $25,000, Total $39,200/month. Current Sales $180,000 (first 6 months). Projections: $1.2M Year 1, $4.5M Year 2, Projected Annual Profit $420,000 Year 1.

Add an interactive app demo section showing a mock phone screen UI with: a food inventory list with items color-coded by expiration status (green = fresh, yellow = expiring soon, red = expired), a sample alerts panel with 3-day and 1-day warnings, and recipe suggestions for expiring food items. The phone mockup should be embedded within the pitch page.

Apply smooth scroll animations and transitions throughout the page: sections should animate in as the user scrolls, feature cards should have hover effects, and the Shark Tank financials section should have a dramatic entrance animation. Ensure the layout is fully responsive across desktop and mobile screen sizes.

Apply a cohesive visual theme throughout: primary greens #2ECC71 and #27AE60, clean white backgrounds, dark charcoal accents, modern sans-serif typography, generous whitespace, and emoji icons used consistently for visual appeal across all sections. The overall aesthetic should feel professional, startup-ready, and investor-facing.
